questionText: Simplify $\dfrac{y^7}{y^2}$, leaving your answer in index form.

steps.explanation: We are dividing two powers of the same base $y$, which the division law of indices handles.
Dividing powers of the same base keeps the base and subtracts the indices, since two factors of $y$ cancel from the seven on top.
Subtracting $2$ from $7$ leaves $5$ factors of $y$ after cancellation.
Placing the difference of indices on the base gives $y^5$.

steps.workingLatex: \dfrac{y \cdot y \cdot y \cdot y \cdot y \cdot y \cdot y}{y \cdot y} = y^5
steps.explanation: Cancelling the two bottom $y$'s against two of the seven on top leaves exactly five, confirming the result.
The simplified expression in index form is $y^5$.
